notes: Buffett's largest single position 2016-2024. Cook's CEO tenure from 2011 — capital return discipline (buybacks at sub-intrinsic multiples), strong owner orientation. Fill rubric after re-reading FY2024 letter + earnings calls.
Cornerstone Berkshire holding since 1988. James Quincey CEO. Long history of dividend discipline + reinvestment in core brands.
Stephen Squeri CEO. Buyback discipline + per-share framing in shareholder letters. Use as positive anchor for capital allocation.
Buffett added through 2022-2023 dip. Vicki Hollub CEO. Operating-driven buybacks; debt paydown focus. Use as positive anchor for capital allocation.
Same-cap-band tech control. NOT a Berkshire/Town pick but high-quality fundamentals — useful to test that 'good fundamentals' alone don't trigger MGT_PASS without owner-orientation evidence.
Matched control — high-growth consumer tech. Buybacks have been opportunistic, not disciplined. Useful negative anchor for capital_allocation sub-check.
Negative anchor — frequent compensation controversies, founder-led but with capital allocation noise. Use to validate that compensation-alignment sub-check fires.
Pre-split GE was a textbook capital-misallocation case. Post-split GE Aerospace has different management. Tag the timestamp explicitly so the snapshot reflects which entity we're evaluating.
